response.justification: Candidate is an experienced full-stack engineer with broad polyglot exposure, but the resume shows no meaningful Kotlin work, no Spring/Quarkus/Hibernate experience, and only marginal Java involvement (robotic arms, mobile orchestration tooling years ago). The role demands deep JVM expertise and enterprise framework fluency, which are not evidenced. Prior IDE-adjacent work at National Instruments on LabWindows/CVI is in C/C++, not JVM tooling. Fit for this specific JVM/IDE position is weak despite solid general engineering credentials.
strengths: Long track record building complex systems across web, mobile, embedded
Prior IDE component experience at National Instruments (LabWindows/CVI)
Strong testing/TDD culture and developer-tooling mindset
concerns: No demonstrated Spring, Quarkus, or Hibernate experience
Java listed as a skill but used sparingly; no Kotlin or deep JVM internals shown
Core stack is TypeScript/Node and DLang, not the JVM ecosystem required
